zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Way於1582年3月10日出生在英國多瑟特包維爾斯托可時，他的父親George Way29歲，母親Agnes Symes23歲。他於大約1607年與Elizabeth Walker在英國英格蘭多瑟特包維爾斯托可結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1666年3月24日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可格洛托享年84歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Way于1582年3月10日出生在英国多瑟特包維爾斯托可时，他的父亲George Way29岁，母亲Agnes Symes23岁。他于大约1607年与Elizabeth Walker在英国英格兰多瑟特包維爾斯托可结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1666年3月24日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可格洛托享年84岁。

nameMeanings.name: Way
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly southern): topographic name for someone who lived near a road or path, from Middle English wei (Old English weg, cognate with Old Norse vegr, Old High German weg), or a habitational name from some minor place called with this word, as for example any of the places called Way or Waye in Devon or Way in Somerset and Kent.

variants.name: Wei
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ming, Wei, Li, Chao, Hong, Cheng, Jin, Ling, Ping, Yuan, Bin, Dong, Lan, Min, Chang, Chong, Chung, Houa, Pai, Sha, Yiming.

Mandarin form of the surname 魏, a term referring to ‘the buildings at either side of the gates of the royal palace’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Wei (魏), originally the name of a fief (located in Ruicheng in Shanxi province) granted to Bi Wan, an official in the state of Jin. In 403  BC , the state of Jin was split into three, one of which was the state of Wei (魏), established by Wei Huan Zi (魏桓子). After this state was annexed by the state of Qin in 225  BC , its name was adopted by some people as a surname. (ii) adopted in place of the surname Mi (羋) by Wei Ran (魏冉), an official who lived in the state of Qin during the Warring States period (475–221  BC ). (iii) borne by descendants of Wei Liao Weng (魏了翁), a scholar during the Southern Song dynasty (1127–1279  AD ), who changed his original name Gao (高) to his uncle's surname Wei (魏).

variants.name: Wey
meanings: English (Dorset and Somerset): variant of   Way  .
Dutch: variant, mostly archaic or Americanized, of Weij, a topographic name from weide ‘pasture, meadow’ (compare Vanderweide ).
German: from a short form of any of various ancient Germanic personal names based on the element wīg ‘battle’ (compare Weyers ). This surname is also found in Belgium.

variants.name: Waye
meanings: English (Devon and Kent): variant of   Way  .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Way was born on 10 March 1582, in Powerstock, Dorset, England, his father, George Way, was 29 and his mother, Agnes Symes, was 23. He married Elizabeth Walker about 1607, in Powerstock, Dorset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 24 March 1666, in Groton, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 84.

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Way(이)가 1582년 3월 10일 영국 도르세트 파워스톡에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 George Way(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Symes(은)는 23세였다. 그는 대략 1607년 영국 잉글랜드 도르세트 파워스톡에서 Elizabeth Walker(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1666년 3월 24일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 그로턴에서 84세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.Groton, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Col: гротон, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Way родился 10 марта 1582 г. в местности павэрстокк, дорсет, Англия, его отцу по имени George Way было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes Symes, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Walker около 1607 г. в местности павэрстокк, дорсет, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер 24 марта 1666 г. в местности гротон, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 84 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Way nació el 10 de marzo de 1582, en Powerstock, Dorset, Inglaterra, su papá, George Way, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Agnes Symes, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Walker aproximadamente 1607, en Powerstock, Dorset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 24 de marzo de 1666, en Groton, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 84 años.
